title: Quality control and planning engineers
description: Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems. Develop plans for surface transportation projects, according to established engineering standards and state or federal construction policy. Prepare designs, specifications, or estimates for transportation facilities. Plan modifications of existing streets, highways, or freeways to improve traffic flow. (Based on 42 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Simulate reservoir performance for different recovery techniques using computer models.
Design mining and mineral treatment equipment and machinery in collaboration with other engineering specialists.
Analyze data to recommend placement of wells and supplementary processes to enhance production.

Develop plans for new water resources or water efficiency programs based on technical assessments.

Design and prepare plans for new transportation systems or parts of systems like highways, bridges, or drainage structures.

Prepare or present reports on environmental impact statements, public hearings, or contamination tests related to construction projects.

Collect and analyze pollution samples, such as air or water, to assess environmental compliance and safety standards.
Analyze and recommend methods for wastewater treatment and design water distribution systems for potable or non-potable use.
Test materials and products for adherence to specifications, performance characteristics, and quality assurance standards.
Train personnel on technical methods in engineering or forensic science, while planning work assignments in accordance with performance schedules.
Assist in the cleanup of hazardous material spills.
Oversee support staff involved in quality control activities.
Improve chemical processes to reduce toxic emissions in production methods.
Analyze data related to traffic flow and accident rates to determine methods to expedite traffic flow.

Prepare capability data, training materials, or other documentation for transfer of processes to production.

Create or maintain formal engineering documents, such as schematics, bills of materials, or specifications.
Prepare technical reports and data summaries for scientific publication, regulatory submissions, or patent applications.
Conduct worker training in areas such as safety laws and regulations, and monitor compliance with these regulations.
Develop methodologies for transferring procedures from laboratories to commercial-scale manufacturing production.
Investigate industrial accidents or injuries to determine causes and recommend preventive measures.

Review existing manufacturing processes to identify opportunities for yield improvement or to ensure conformance to specifications.
Conduct analyses addressing issues such as failure, reliability, or yield improvement, and interpret safety regulations.
Research new materials and apply them to product designs, such as implanted artificial organs or biomedical equipment.

Supervise and participate in inspections of properties to ensure compliance with fire codes, ordinances, laws, and regulations.
